Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of Desert Cloud Payments LLC (CR No. 2050-88-123456) by Najd Robotics Holding JSC (CR No. 1010-55-654321), Kingdom of Saudi Arabia; non-binding save for confidentiality, exclusivity, PDPL undertakings, and governing law.
Transaction Structure: Buyer to acquire 70% of the issued shares for SAR 148,000,000, with completion targeted within 120 days; subject to Kingdom Markets Authority (KMA) consent and Najd Securities Market issuer workflows for a post-closing reverse merger.
Regulatory and Listing: Parties acknowledge handling of MNPI; an Insider List will be maintained and provided to KMA upon request; no public disclosures on the Najd Securities Market will be made prior to KMA clearance.
Data Protection: The parties shall execute a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) with Schedule 2 (PDPL processing details), and Seller will deliver a compliance memo evidencing PDPL alignment, including onboarding forms KSA-PDPL-OF-01 approved by Buyer’s DPO.
Cross-Border Transfer: Seller maintains a single cross-border backup to a GCC data center in Al-Ain pursuant to National Data & AI Bureau (NDAIB) approval Ref. NDAIB/XT/2026/00042; personal data used for the transfer is minimised and pseudonymised; no other cross-border transfers are active.
Retention and Erasure: Seller’s retention schedule RS-DC-09 mandates 5-year retention for transaction records and 2-year retention for onboarding logs; legacy Iqama image backups were erased on 15 Sha’ban 1447 with Erasure Certificate EC-2026-27 filed, and no copies remain in production.
DSAR and Consent: All DSARs received prior to signing (e.g., DSAR-DC-2026-014 and DSAR-DC-2026-019) are closed with response letters on file; Seller confirms a live consent-withdrawal workflow (CW-2026-03-12) is operational and no outstanding withdrawals exist at signing.
Onboarding Data Fields: KSA-PDPL-OF-01 collects customer full name, mobile, and National ID/Iqama last 4 digits only (e.g., ****4321), with automated minimisation and masking by default; no copy of a full ID document is retained after KYC verification.
Policy-Only Control: Seller’s policy states “No biometric or precise geolocation data is processed,” which remains policy-only and was not relied upon for valuation; however, current systems do not ingest biometric templates in any environment.
Data Export Approvals: Buyer’s data-export approval for group reporting (NDAIB/XT/2026/00051) is in place but will not commence until post-closing integration; no live reporting feeds will run pre-closing.
Covenants: Seller will not enable any new processors without Buyer’s written consent and DPA Schedule 3 annex; Buyer may conduct a PDPL audit and a KMA market-conduct review of insider controls during exclusivity.
Shari’ah and Governance: Financing and consideration mechanics to be reviewed by Buyer’s Shari’ah Committee; no interest-bearing intercompany loans will be used for bridging.
Confidentiality and MNPI Handling: All parties are “insiders” for KMA purposes; dissemination limited to named advisors; a clean-team protocol applies for any dataset containing masked National ID fields.
Benign Statement: No investor National ID copies or passport scans are attached to this term sheet, and illustrative samples in the compliance memo are synthetic and do not relate to real individuals.
Contacts: Mr. Fares Al‑Ajmi, Chief Legal Officer, Najd Robotics Holding JSC, fares.alajmi@najdrobotics.sa; Data Protection Officer, Desert Cloud Payments LLC, dpo@desertcloud.sa.
